data.title: Industrias Peñoles, S.A.B. de C.V.'s (BMV:PE&OLES) Stock Is Going Strong: Is the Market Following Fundamentals?
data.description:
Industrias Peñoles. de's (BMV:PE&OLES) stock is up by a considerable 43% over the past three months. Since the market usually pay for a company’s long-term fundamentals, we decided to study the company’s key performance indicators to see if they could be influencing the market. Specifically, we decided to study Industrias Peñoles. de's ROE in this article.

Return on equity or ROE is an important factor to be considered by a shareholder because it tells them how effectively their capital is being reinvested. In simpler terms, it measures the profitability of a company in relation to shareholder's equity.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

How Is ROE Calculated?

ROE can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for Industrias Peñoles. de is:

18% = US$1.2b ÷ US$6.5b (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' is the yearly profit. That means that for every MX$1 worth of shareholders' equity, the company generated MX$0.18 in profit.

Check out our latest analysis for Industrias Peñoles. de

Why Is ROE Important For Earnings Growth?

We have already established that ROE serves as an efficient profit-generating gauge for a company's future earnings. Based on how much of its profits the company chooses to reinvest or "retain", we are then able to evaluate a company's future ability to generate profits. Assuming everything else remains unchanged, the higher the ROE and profit retention, the higher the growth rate of a company compared to companies that don't necessarily bear these characteristics.

A Side By Side comparison of Industrias Peñoles. de's Earnings Growth And 18% ROE

To begin with, Industrias Peñoles. de seems to have a respectable ROE. On comparing with the average industry ROE of 15% the company's ROE looks pretty remarkable. This probably laid the ground for Industrias Peñoles. de's moderate 17% net income growth seen over the past five years.

We then compared Industrias Peñoles. de's net income growth with the industry and we're pleased to see that the company's growth figure is higher when compared with the industry which has a growth rate of 0.8% in the same 5-year period.

The basis for attaching value to a company is, to a great extent, tied to its earnings growth. What investors need to determine next is if the expected earnings growth, or the lack of it, is already built into the share price. Doing so will help them establish if the stock's future looks promising or ominous. One good indicator of expected earnings growth is the P/E ratio which determines the price the market is willing to pay for a stock based on its earnings prospects. So, you may want to check if Industrias Peñoles. de is trading on a high P/E or a low P/E, relative to its industry.

Is Industrias Peñoles. de Efficiently Re-investing Its Profits?

While the company did pay out a portion of its dividend in the past, it currently doesn't pay a regular dividend. We infer that the company has been reinvesting all of its profits to grow its business.

Summary

Overall, we are quite pleased with Industrias Peñoles. de's performance. Particularly, we like that the company is reinvesting heavily into its business, and at a high rate of return. Unsurprisingly, this has led to an impressive earnings growth. We also studied the latest analyst forecasts and found that the company's earnings growth is expected be similar to its current growth rate. To know more about the company's future earnings growth forecasts take a look at this free report on analyst forecasts for the company to find out more.

data.title: Is Leidos’ Growing Federal Backlog Reframing the Long‑Term Investment Case for LDOS?
data.description:
• Leidos Holdings has previously prepared to release its fiscal fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on Tuesday, Feb. 17, with analysts expecting higher profitability and closely watching its performance against past earnings beats.
  • An interesting angle for investors is how Leidos’ growing federal contract backlog, supported by elevated U.S. defense and government IT spending, underpins its positioning in cybersecurity and digital modernization.
  • Next, we’ll examine how Leidos’ expanding federal contract backlog shapes the company’s investment narrative for long-term government technology exposure.

What Is Leidos Holdings' Investment Narrative?

For anyone looking at Leidos, the big picture is about steady exposure to long-term U.S. government technology and defense spending, supported by an expanding federal contract backlog and historically high return on equity. The upcoming Q4 2025 earnings on Feb. 17 sit at the center of the short term story, and the recent news mainly reinforces existing catalysts rather than changing them outright: consistent EPS delivery, disciplined capital returns via dividends and buybacks, and solid profit margins. With the share price already up strongly over the past few years and trading below many fair value estimates, expectations around another earnings beat are a swing factor for near term sentiment. The more persistent risk, however, remains Leidos’ high debt load and its relatively new management team.

However, one key balance sheet issue may matter more than recent contract wins.

data.title: How Record Q3 Results And FDA-Cleared MRI Pump Launch At IRADIMED (IRMD) Has Changed Its Investment Story
data.description:
• Riverwater Partners recently highlighted IRADIMED CORPORATION as the top contributor to its Micro Opportunities Strategy in 2025, following record Q3 2025 revenue of US$21.2 million and FDA approval of the company’s MRI-compatible 3870 IV infusion pump in July 2025.
  • Beyond the financial headlines, IRADIMED’s combination of accelerating revenue, new FDA-cleared technology and a special dividend, alongside insider share sales and a “Moderate Buy” analyst consensus, paints a complex picture of how management, investors and analysts are responding to the company’s progress.
  • With this backdrop, we’ll examine how the FDA-cleared MRI-compatible 3870 IV infusion pump shapes IRADIMED’s investment narrative and future prospects.

What Is IRADIMED's Investment Narrative?

To own IRADIMED, you have to believe that its niche in MRI-compatible devices can support durable demand, and that the newly approved 3870 IV infusion pump becomes a meaningful part of that story rather than a one-off headline. The recent Riverwater Partners highlight, strong one-year share price gains and record Q3 2025 results all reinforce the pump’s role as a short term catalyst, especially as management has already raised full year revenue and EPS guidance and backed that confidence with both a higher regular dividend and a special payout. At the same time, a rich valuation, insider selling and a “Moderate Buy” consensus suggest expectations are already high, so any stumble in commercialization, slower revenue growth or execution issues around the 3870 could quickly shift sentiment.

However, the current valuation and recent insider selling introduce a risk investors should not ignore. IRADIMED's shares are on the way up, but could they be overextended? Uncover how much higher they are than fair value.

data.title: Returns On Capital At Aguas Andinas (SNSE:AGUAS-A) Have Stalled
data.description:
There are a few key trends to look for if we want to identify the next multi-bagger. Firstly, we'd want to identify a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and then alongside that, an ever-increasing base of capital employed. This shows us that it's a compounding machine, able to continually reinvest its earnings back into the business and generate higher returns. However, after briefly looking over the numbers, we don't think Aguas Andinas (SNSE:AGUAS-A) has the makings of a multi-bagger going forward, but let's have a look at why that may be.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

Return On Capital Employed (ROCE): What Is It?

For those that aren't sure what ROCE is, it measures the amount of pre-tax profits a company can generate from the capital employed in its business. The formula for this calculation on Aguas Andinas is:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.089 = CL$259b ÷ (CL$3.2t - CL$260b) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

So, Aguas Andinas has an ROCE of 8.9%. On its own that's a low return on capital but it's in line with the industry's average returns of 8.9%.

See our latest analysis for Aguas Andinas

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Aguas Andinas compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering Aguas Andinas for free.

How Are Returns Trending?

The returns on capital haven't changed much for Aguas Andinas in recent years. Over the past five years, ROCE has remained relatively flat at around 8.9% and the business has deployed 46% more capital into its operations. Given the company has increased the amount of capital employed, it appears the investments that have been made simply don't provide a high return on capital.

What We Can Learn From Aguas Andinas' ROCE

Long story short, while Aguas Andinas has been reinvesting its capital, the returns that it's generating haven't increased. Investors must think there's better things to come because the stock has knocked it out of the park, delivering a 138% gain to shareholders who have held over the last five years. But if the trajectory of these underlying trends continue, we think the likelihood of it being a multi-bagger from here isn't high.

On a separate note, we've found 2 warning signs for Aguas Andinas you'll probably want to know about.

For those who like to invest in solid companies, check out this free list of companies with solid balance sheets and high returns on equity.

data.title: Cardinal Energy (TSE:CJ) Will Pay A Dividend Of CA$0.06
data.description:
The board of Cardinal Energy Ltd. (TSE:CJ) has announced that it will pay a dividend on the 17th of February, with investors receiving CA$0.06 per share. Based on this payment, the dividend yield on the company's stock will be 7.9%, which is an attractive boost to shareholder returns.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Cardinal Energy's Projections Indicate Future Payments May Be Unsustainable

Impressive dividend yields are good, but this doesn't matter much if the payments can't be sustained. Before this announcement, Cardinal Energy was paying out 150% of what it was earning, and not generating any free cash flows either. Paying out such a large dividend compared to earnings while also not generating any free cash flow would definitely be difficult to keep up.

Looking forward, earnings per share is forecast to fall by 15.9% over the next year. Assuming the dividend continues along recent trends, we believe the payout ratio could reach 180%, which could put the dividend under pressure if earnings don't start to improve.

View our latest analysis for Cardinal Energy

Dividend Volatility

While the company has been paying a dividend for a long time, it has cut the dividend at least once in the last 10 years. The dividend has gone from an annual total of CA$0.84 in 2016 to the most recent total annual payment of CA$0.72. The dividend has shrunk at around 1.5% a year during that period. A company that decreases its dividend over time generally isn't what we are looking for.

Dividend Growth Could Be Constrained

Growing earnings per share could be a mitigating factor when considering the past fluctuations in the dividend. Cardinal Energy has impressed us by growing EPS at 17% per year over the past five years. However, the company isn't reinvesting a lot back into the business, so we would expect the growth rate to slow down somewhat in the future.

Cardinal Energy's Dividend Doesn't Look Sustainable

In summary, while it's good to see that the dividend hasn't been cut, we are a bit cautious about Cardinal Energy's payments, as there could be some issues with sustaining them into the future. In general, the distributions are a little bit higher than we would like, but we can't ignore the fact the quickly growing earnings gives this stock great potential in the future. We don't think Cardinal Energy is a great stock to add to your portfolio if income is your focus.

Companies possessing a stable dividend policy will likely enjoy greater investor interest than those suffering from a more inconsistent approach. At the same time, there are other factors our readers should be conscious of before pouring capital into a stock. For example, we've identified 3 warning signs for Cardinal Energy (2 make us uncomfortable!) that you should be aware of before investing. If you are a dividend investor, you might also want to look at our curated list of high yield dividend stocks.

data.title: Alamos Gold Halcon Discovery Reshapes Mulatos Growth And Valuation Story
data.description:
• Alamos Gold (TSX:AGI) reported a new exploration discovery at the Halcon target within the Mulatos District.
  • The company identified significant sulphide-hosted, high grade gold mineralization that extends beyond existing reserves.
  • Alamos Gold is also progressing with construction of a mill designed to process sulphide ore at its PDA project.

For investors watching Alamos Gold at a current share price of CA$55.55, this Halcon discovery adds a fresh element to the story. The stock has risen 92.0% over the past year and has delivered a very large return over five years, which means expectations around new resource potential may already be part of the conversation. Against that backdrop, a new zone of high grade mineralization can be an important operational development to track.

The construction of a dedicated sulphide mill at PDA, alongside the Halcon results, suggests a company preparing for a different mix of ore sources within the Mulatos District. As future drilling, engineering work, and project updates come through, the key question for you as an investor is how these pieces might fit together within Alamos Gold's longer term production and capital plans.

data.title: Nvidia Faces China Chip Disruption While Expanding AI And Healthcare Reach
data.description:
• Chinese customs authorities have blocked shipments of Nvidia's H200 AI processors into China, prompting suppliers to pause production and disrupting supply of these chips.
  • CEO Jensen Huang is planning a late January visit to China as trade tensions and export policy adjustments create uncertainty for NasdaqGS:NVDA in this key market.
  • Nvidia is also expanding its AI footprint through new partnerships, including a US$1b co innovation lab with Eli Lilly and collaborations in life sciences and robotics.

Nvidia, traded as NasdaqGS:NVDA, is a central player in AI computing, with its GPUs widely used in data centers, research, and advanced automation. The current supply chain disruption for H200 chips in China coincides with ongoing trade frictions and tighter export rules, directly affecting a region that has been an important outlet for its AI hardware. At the same time, the company is expanding into healthcare and robotics through new AI partnerships that focus on infrastructure and real world applications.

For investors, these developments highlight both operational risk in a major market and potential opportunity in higher value AI ecosystems. Jensen Huang's upcoming China trip underscores the importance of regulatory clarity and business continuity in the region, while the Eli Lilly co innovation lab and other alliances illustrate how Nvidia is positioning its technology across drug discovery, diagnostics, and automation over an extended time frame.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for NVIDIA by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on NVIDIA.

Is NVIDIA financially strong enough to weather the next crisis?

The H200 blockage in China puts a spotlight on how much regulatory friction can affect where Nvidia’s data center revenue comes from, especially given prior expectations for more than 1,000,000 H200 orders in that market. At the same time, Jensen Huang’s China visit and the US$1b Eli Lilly co innovation lab, plus a cluster of new healthcare and robotics collaborations, suggest Nvidia is putting more emphasis on AI ecosystems and geographically diverse demand rather than relying on any single country.

Advertisement

How This Fits, Or Clashes, With The Usual NVIDIA Narrative

Nvidia is often described as an AI infrastructure pure play tied closely to hyperscalers and China exposure, so customs blocks on H200 processors and talk of new US oversight on advanced AI exports directly test that storyline. The recent MoUs and deals with Eli Lilly, Thermo Fisher, Natera, Multiply Labs, Freenome and robotics partners broaden the narrative from one focused mainly on training chips in big tech data centers to one where Nvidia’s hardware and software sit inside regulated healthcare and industrial workflows as well.

Risks and rewards investors should weigh

  • ⚠️ Export controls, tariffs and a potential two year ban on Blackwell class exports to China could limit access to a historically important market and introduce more policy headline risk.
  • ⚠️ H200 shipment halts and customs uncertainty raise the chance of delayed orders, supply chain inefficiencies and pressure to redesign products for specific jurisdictions.
  • 🎁 Healthcare and life sciences alliances, including the co innovation lab with Eli Lilly and expanded BioNeMo offering, create new use cases for Nvidia’s AI stack in large, regulated end markets.
  • 🎁 Partnerships across robotics, quantum links, data center operators and software platforms can deepen Nvidia’s role as a full stack AI provider, not just a chip vendor, which some investors view as a support for more durable revenue streams.

What to watch next

From here, keep an eye on how quickly customs issues around the H200 are clarified, whether any formal Chinese import decision is announced, and how export legislation on advanced chips evolves in the US and key allies. It is also worth tracking deal flow and product adoption inside the new healthcare and robotics partnerships, since consistent contract wins there could offset any regional pressure on data center hardware. You can also stay on top of how these themes feed into sentiment by checking out the latest community views in our AI and semiconductor narratives hub.

data.title: A Look At Sprott’s Valuation As Silver Trust Ups ATM Capacity To US$2.0b
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Sprott’s updated silver trust funding move matters

Sprott (TSX:SII) drew fresh investor interest after its subsidiary, Sprott Asset Management LP, updated the at the market equity program for Sprott Physical Silver Trust to allow issuance of up to US$2.0b in units.

The Trust plans to use any proceeds to acquire additional physical silver bullion. This directly links this capital raising capacity to potential growth in assets under management and future fee revenue for Sprott’s broader asset management platform.

See our latest analysis for Sprott.

The updated funding capacity arrives after a strong run in Sprott’s shares, with a 27.21% 1 month share price return and 54.01% 3 month share price return, alongside a very large 1 year total shareholder return. Together, these figures indicate that positive momentum has recently been building, as investors reassess both growth potential and risk around its exposure to precious metals and related products.

If this kind of move has your attention, it could be a good moment to broaden your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Sprott shares up sharply over 1 month, 3 months and 1 year, and trading above the CA$138.50 analyst price target and an intrinsic value estimate, you have to ask whether there is still a buying opportunity here or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Price-to-Earnings of 65x: Is it justified?

At the last close of CA$175.46, Sprott is trading on a P/E of 65x, which screens as expensive compared with both peers and its industry.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price to earnings per share, so a higher multiple usually reflects investors paying more today for each dollar of earnings. For an asset management holding company like Sprott, a rich P/E can signal that the market is placing a high value on its fee streams, growth optionality and exposure to precious metals related products.

Here, the data flags that the market is paying a much higher multiple of earnings than is typical. Sprott is described as expensive on a 65x P/E versus a peer average of 25.5x, and also expensive versus the Canadian Capital Markets industry average P/E of 9.5x. That is a sizeable gap and suggests expectations for the business sit well above the broader group.

Against peers, a 65x P/E stands out as a premium that is hard to ignore, given the 25.5x peer average and 9.5x industry level are both far lower comparison points.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 65x (OVERVALUED)

However, there are clear risks to keep in mind, including Sprott’s premium 65x P/E versus peers and its concentrated exposure to precious metals-related products.

Find out about the key risks to this Sprott narrative.

Another way to look at value

Our DCF model approaches Sprott from a very different angle to the 65x P/E. On this view, the current price of CA$175.46 sits well above an estimated future cash flow value of CA$39.28, which suggests that Sprott appears very expensive on cash flow assumptions as well. If both earnings and cash flow views are this stretched, it may be worth considering how comfortable you are with paying that kind of premium.

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Sprott for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 876 undervalued stocks based on their cash flows. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

Build Your Own Sprott Narrative

If you see the numbers differently or prefer to rely on your own work, you can create a fresh view in just a few minutes, starting with Do it your way.

A good starting point is our analysis highlighting 2 key rewards investors are optimistic about regarding Sprott.

data.title: Could APA (APA) Offer Value After Recent Share Price Gains?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether APA is priced attractively right now, this article walks through what the current share price might imply about the company’s value.
  • APA closed at US$26.54, with returns of 8.7% over the last 30 days, 4.7% year to date, 19.5% over 1 year, a 32.6% decline over 3 years, and 109.2% over 5 years. Together, these figures give a mixed picture of recent market sentiment.
  • Recent coverage around APA has focused on its position in the US energy sector and how investors are weighing sector specific risks and opportunities against individual company fundamentals. This context helps explain why the share price has seen both shorter term gains and longer term share price changes.
  • On Simply Wall St’s valuation checks, APA scores 5 out of 6 for being assessed as undervalued. Next, we will look at how different valuation approaches line up for the stock and finish with one approach that ties them together in a more holistic way.

APA delivered 19.5% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Oil and Gas industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: APA Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a single present value figure.

For APA, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $1.96b. Analyst inputs and extrapolated estimates suggest projected free cash flows through to 2035, including a forecast of $2.16b in 2030. Simply Wall St notes that analyst forecasts typically cover up to five years, and the later years are extrapolated from those inputs.

Bringing all those projected cash flows back to today yields an estimated intrinsic value of $110.11 per share. At the recent share price of $26.54, the DCF output implies APA trades at a 75.9% discount to this estimate. On these assumptions, the shares appear to be priced well below the modelled intrinsic value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests APA is undervalued by 75.9%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 876 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for APA.

Approach 2: APA Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like APA, the P/E ratio is a useful yardstick because it links what you pay today to the earnings the business is already generating. It helps you see how many dollars investors are currently willing to pay for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk profile. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk usually support a higher P/E, while more uncertainty or lower expected growth tends to pull that multiple down.

APA currently trades on a P/E of 6.24x. That sits below the Oil and Gas industry average P/E of 13.51x and also below the broader peer average of 23.91x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for APA is 15.00x, which is its proprietary view of what a fitting P/E could be after factoring in elements such as earnings growth characteristics, profit margin, industry, market cap and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the sector because it is tailored to APA’s own profile rather than a broad group average. With the Fair Ratio of 15.00x above the current 6.24x, APA screens as undervalued on this P/E based approach.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1444 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your APA Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives.

A Narrative is simply your story about APA, written in numbers, where you set your own view of fair value along with expectations for future revenue, earnings and margins instead of only relying on a single model or market multiple.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives live in the Community page and link APA’s story to a full financial forecast and then to a fair value. This allows you to quickly compare that fair value to today’s share price to help decide whether APA looks attractive, fully priced or expensive to you at any given time.

Narratives are updated automatically when new data such as news or earnings is released. You might see one investor running a very cautious APA Narrative with lower revenue growth and margins, while another uses higher growth and profitability assumptions for a higher fair value, giving you a clear sense of the range of views in the market.

Do you think there's more to the story for APA? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Assessing Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) Valuation After VK2735 Phase 2 Success And Phase 3 Trial Progress
data.description:
Advertisement

Why VK2735’s latest trial results matter for Viking Therapeutics (VKTX)

Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) is back in focus after publishing Phase 2 VENTURE trial results for its dual GLP-1/GIP candidate VK2735 and confirming full enrollment in two large Phase 3 obesity and diabetes studies.

See our latest analysis for Viking Therapeutics.

VKTX’s share price has been choppy around the latest VK2735 updates, with a 7 day share price return of 4.71% but a 30 day share price return of 8.32% and a year to date share price return of a 5.76% decline, while the 3 year total shareholder return of almost 3x suggests longer term momentum has been strong even if recent gains have cooled.

If obesity drug news has caught your attention, it could be a good moment to see how Viking compares with other healthcare stocks that are also trying to reshape treatment options.

With VKTX trading at about US$33.38 and an implied discount to some analyst targets plus an intrinsic value gap, you have to ask: is Viking quietly undervalued here, or is the market already baking in future growth?

Price-to-book of 5.3x, is it justified?

On a P/B basis, Viking trades at 5.3x, while our DCF model points to a fair value of about US$276.53 per share versus the last close of US$33.38.

The P/B ratio compares the company’s market value to its book value. It is often used for asset light, early stage biopharma names that are not yet generating meaningful revenue. At 5.3x, investors are effectively paying several times Viking’s accounting equity for exposure to its research pipeline rather than current earnings.

Compared with peers, Viking’s 5.3x P/B is above both the peer group average of 5x and the broader US Biotechs industry average of 2.7x. This suggests the market is assigning a richer valuation relative to balance sheet value than it does for many competitors.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-book of 5.3x (OVERVALUED)

However, you also have to weigh binary clinical outcomes and ongoing net losses of US$237.394 million, which could both shift sentiment around that apparent valuation gap.

Another view: what the DCF model suggests

While the 5.3x P/B ratio makes VKTX look expensive against peers, our DCF model paints a very different picture. On that approach, VKTX at about US$33.38 is trading roughly 87.9% below an implied fair value of around US$276.53 per share, which screens as heavily undervalued.

data.title: Boeing Wins Key Orders As Recovery And Cash Flow Story Builds
data.description:
• Ethiopian Airlines has placed a major new aircraft order with Boeing (NYSE:BA), adding fresh demand for the company’s widebody and narrowbody jets.
  • Delta Air Lines has also committed to additional Boeing aircraft, expanding its fleet plans with new long haul and domestic capacity.
  • Boeing has overtaken Airbus in net commercial orders for the first time in seven years, supported by higher 787 Dreamliner production rates and increasing long haul demand.

Boeing sits at the center of global air travel, supplying commercial jets to airlines that want to refresh fleets and plan for long term passenger demand. After several challenging years for both the company and the wider aviation industry, fresh commitments from Ethiopian Airlines and Delta Air Lines indicate that airlines are planning for more long haul and international flying.

For you as an investor, these new orders and higher production levels offer a clearer view of how Boeing’s commercial segment is being used by airlines. The key questions now revolve around how consistently Boeing can deliver aircraft, manage its supply chain and convert this order momentum into steady cash generation over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Boeing by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Boeing.

How Boeing stacks up against its biggest competitors

Boeing’s fresh 787 orders from Ethiopian Airlines and Delta, combined with 600 commercial aircraft deliveries in 2025, point to airlines committing capital to its widebody and narrowbody platforms over many years. For you, that matters because these contracts feed Boeing’s backlog, support its manufacturing ramp on the 787 program and provide more visibility on how the commercial business might absorb fixed costs and support future cash generation.

Advertisement

How This News Fits Into The Boeing Narrative

Recent commentary has framed Boeing as a recovery and cash flow story, with investors focused less on near term earnings and more on the company’s ability to stabilize production and deliveries. The new orders, the tie up with Salient Motion to speed up certification of motion control systems and evidence of higher Dreamlifter activity all add detail to that narrative by showing where operational improvements and product demand are intersecting.

Risks and Rewards To Keep In Mind

  • Large 787 and 737 MAX commitments from Delta and Ethiopian add to Boeing’s order book and may support long term production planning.
  • Growing 787 output and partnerships like Salient Motion’s modular actuation platform could help reduce unit costs and shorten development timelines.
  • Boeing still faces scrutiny over manufacturing quality and certification processes, so any setbacks could affect deliveries and customer confidence.
  • Trade and tariff discussions that explicitly reference Boeing aircraft create an external risk that sits outside operational progress.

What To Watch Next

From here, it is worth watching how consistently Boeing converts this order momentum into aircraft deliveries, how the 787 production rate evolves, and whether new technology partnerships actually speed certification and lower costs. If you want to see how other investors are interpreting this story, check out the latest community narratives on this page and compare how they line up with your own thesis.

data.title: Capital One Balances Settlement Costs, Rate Cap Risk And Discover Deal
data.description:
• Capital One Financial received preliminary court approval for a $425 million class action settlement with depositors over denied high interest rates.
  • The company faces regulatory risk from President Trump's proposed cap on credit card interest rates.
  • Capital One is also progressing with its pending acquisition of Discover, a move with potential implications for competition and its future business mix.

For investors watching NYSE:COF, these developments arrive as the stock trades at $231.01, with a 1 year return of 16.4% and a 3 year return of 109.6%. Over the past month, shares show a 6.4% decline, and year to date the stock is down 6.8%, which indicates that the market may already be reacting to parts of this evolving story.

The combination of a large legal settlement, possible federal limits on credit card interest rates, and the pending Discover deal gives you several moving pieces to assess. How these items are ultimately resolved, including any final settlement terms or regulatory conditions, could affect Capital One Financial's earnings mix, risk profile, and competitive position over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Capital One Financial by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Capital One Financial.

Is Capital One Financial financially strong enough to weather the next crisis?

The preliminary approval of the US$425m settlement looks like a step toward closing off legal uncertainty around past savings account practices, but it still represents a sizeable cash outlay and a reminder of conduct risk in Capital One’s deposit franchise. At the same time, the proposed 10% cap on credit card interest rates strikes at the core of its card focused model, with some analysts flagging at least 10% earnings pressure if it were implemented, which helps explain why recent headlines have fed into share price volatility.

Advertisement

Capital One Financial narrative, tested by regulation and deal execution

For investors who view Capital One as a card centric bank with upside from owning the Discover network, this news flow adds extra conditions to that story, including regulatory scrutiny on pricing and conduct as well as execution risk on integrating an issuer network model. The balance between potential benefits from a closed loop system and the possibility of tighter rules on interest rates and consumer products is likely to be central to how the long term narrative develops.

Risks and rewards in focus

  • ⚠️ The proposed 10% rate cap is described by analysts as a high severity risk for issuers like Capital One that rely heavily on credit card balances and net interest income.
  • ⚠️ The US$425m settlement highlights ongoing conduct and legal risk, even as it removes the prospect of a separate action from New York’s attorney general if finally approved.
  • 🎁 The pending Discover acquisition, if completed on current terms, could cut reliance on external networks and reshape margins across the card business.
  • 🎁 Several brokers have recently raised price targets and kept positive or neutral recommendations, which shows that some market participants still see a constructive case despite the regulatory overhang.

What to watch next

Looking ahead, you may want to watch three things in particular: final court approval of the settlement and any follow on conduct requirements; how regulators and courts respond to the proposed 10% cap; and the degree of clarity management provides on Discover integration, card pricing and capital returns on upcoming calls. If you want to see how other investors are framing these moving parts in their own words, you can check out community narratives on this dedicated page and compare different angles on the Capital One story.

data.title: A Look At Lamb Weston (LW) Valuation After Focus To Win Plant Restructuring
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Lamb Weston’s latest plant moves matter for investors

Lamb Weston Holdings (LW) is closing its Munro plant in Argentina, shifting Latin American production to a newer facility in Mar del Plata and temporarily curtailing a line in the Netherlands, all under its Focus to Win program.

See our latest analysis for Lamb Weston Holdings.

The Focus to Win plant changes come after a tough stretch for holders, with a 90 day share price return of a 31.98% decline and a 1 year total shareholder return of a 25.30% decline. This comes even though near term share price momentum has picked up, with a 30 day share price return of 3.55% and a year to date share price return of 4.8%.

If this sort of operational reshuffle has you rethinking your watchlist, it could be worth scanning fast growing stocks with high insider ownership as a fresh source of ideas beyond the usual names.

After a share price that is still 25.30% lower over 1 year but has risen 4.8% year to date, along with a value score of 5 and an indicated intrinsic discount, is Lamb Weston mispriced or already reflecting its future growth?

Price-to-Earnings of 15.7x: Is it justified?

At a last close of US$44.32, Lamb Weston is trading on a P/E of 15.7x that screens as good value relative to both peers and its own fair ratio.

The P/E multiple compares the current share price to earnings per share, so you are essentially paying 15.7 years of current earnings for each share. For a branded frozen foods business with positive earnings and analyst forecasts for profit growth, this metric is a straightforward way to see what the market is willing to pay for those earnings today.

According to the Simply Wall St checks, Lamb Weston’s P/E of 15.7x is below the US Food industry average of 21.4x and below the peer group average of 16.1x, which points to a cheaper earnings tag than many close comparables. It also sits under the estimated fair P/E ratio of 20.8x, a level the market could eventually move toward if current earnings quality and growth trends remain in focus.

data.title: Gulf Warehousing Company Q.P.S.C (DSM:GWCS) Has Affirmed Its Dividend Of QAR0.10
data.description:
The board of Gulf Warehousing Company Q.P.S.C. (DSM:GWCS) has announced that it will pay a dividend of QAR0.10 per share on the 1st of January. The dividend yield will be 4.3% based on this payment which is still above the industry average.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

Gulf Warehousing Company Q.P.S.C's Projected Earnings Seem Likely To Cover Future Distributions

We like to see robust dividend yields, but that doesn't matter if the payment isn't sustainable. Prior to this announcement, Gulf Warehousing Company Q.P.S.C's dividend was comfortably covered by both cash flow and earnings. This means that a large portion of its earnings are being retained to grow the business.

Over the next year, EPS is forecast to expand by 12.8%. Assuming the dividend continues along recent trends, we think the payout ratio could be 41% by next year, which is in a pretty sustainable range.

See our latest analysis for Gulf Warehousing Company Q.P.S.C

Dividend Volatility

Although the company has a long dividend history, it has been cut at least once in the last 10 years. The dividend has gone from an annual total of QAR0.15 in 2016 to the most recent total annual payment of QAR0.10. This works out to be a decline of approximately 4.0% per year over that time. Declining dividends isn't generally what we look for as they can indicate that the company is running into some challenges.

Dividend Growth Potential Is Shaky

With a relatively unstable dividend, it's even more important to see if earnings per share is growing. Earnings per share has been sinking by 13% over the last five years. Such rapid declines definitely have the potential to constrain dividend payments if the trend continues into the future. On the bright side, earnings are predicted to gain some ground over the next year, but until this turns into a pattern we wouldn't be feeling too comfortable.

Our Thoughts On Gulf Warehousing Company Q.P.S.C's Dividend

Overall, it's nice to see a consistent dividend payment, but we think that longer term, the current level of payment might be unsustainable. The company is generating plenty of cash, which could maintain the dividend for a while, but the track record hasn't been great. We don't think Gulf Warehousing Company Q.P.S.C is a great stock to add to your portfolio if income is your focus.

Market movements attest to how highly valued a consistent dividend policy is compared to one which is more unpredictable. Still, investors need to consider a host of other factors, apart from dividend payments, when analysing a company. For example, we've identified 3 warning signs for Gulf Warehousing Company Q.P.S.C (1 shouldn't be ignored!) that you should be aware of before investing. Looking for more high-yielding dividend ideas? Try our collection of strong dividend payers.

data.title: CTS (NYSE:CTS) Might Have The Makings Of A Multi-Bagger
data.description:
If we want to find a potential multi-bagger, often there are underlying trends that can provide clues. Amongst other things, we'll want to see two things; firstly, a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and secondly, an expansion in the company's amount of capital employed. Ultimately, this demonstrates that it's a business that is reinvesting profits at increasing rates of return. So on that note, CTS (NYSE:CTS) looks quite promising in regards to its trends of return on capital.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Understanding Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)

For those that aren't sure what ROCE is, it measures the amount of pre-tax profits a company can generate from the capital employed in its business. Analysts use this formula to calculate it for CTS:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.12 = US$81m ÷ (US$790m - US$105m) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Therefore, CTS has an ROCE of 12%. In absolute terms, that's a satisfactory return, but compared to the Electronic industry average of 9.0% it's much better.

Check out our latest analysis for CTS

In the above chart we have measured CTS' prior ROCE against its prior performance, but the future is arguably more important. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering CTS for free.

What The Trend Of ROCE Can Tell Us

We like the trends that we're seeing from CTS. Over the last five years, returns on capital employed have risen substantially to 12%. The company is effectively making more money per dollar of capital used, and it's worth noting that the amount of capital has increased too, by 22%. The increasing returns on a growing amount of capital is common amongst multi-baggers and that's why we're impressed.

Our Take On CTS' ROCE

In summary, it's great to see that CTS can compound returns by consistently reinvesting capital at increasing rates of return, because these are some of the key ingredients of those highly sought after multi-baggers. And investors seem to expect more of this going forward, since the stock has rewarded shareholders with a 69% return over the last five years. With that being said, we still think the promising fundamentals mean the company deserves some further due diligence.

On the other side of ROCE, we have to consider valuation. That's why we have a FREE intrinsic value estimation for CTS on our platform that is definitely worth checking out.

If you want to search for solid companies with great earnings, check out this free list of companies with good balance sheets and impressive returns on equity.

Ensign Energy Services (TSX:ESI)

Simply Wall St Financial Health Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Ensign Energy Services Inc., along with its subsidiaries, offers oilfield services to the oil and natural gas industries across Canada, the United States, and internationally, with a market cap of CA$536.25 million.

Operations: The company generates CA$1.65 billion in revenue from oilfield services provided to the oil and natural gas sectors.

Market Cap: CA$536.25M

Ensign Energy Services, with a market cap of CA$536.25 million, is trading significantly below its estimated fair value and offers good relative value compared to peers. Despite being unprofitable, Ensign has reduced its losses over the past five years and maintains a positive free cash flow, ensuring a cash runway of over three years. However, challenges include high net debt to equity at 70.5% and short-term assets not covering long-term liabilities of CA$1.1 billion. Recent earnings reports show declining sales and net losses for Q3 2025 compared to the previous year, reflecting ongoing financial hurdles.

McCoy Global (TSX:MCB)

Simply Wall St Financial Health Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: McCoy Global Inc. offers equipment and technologies for tubular running operations to improve wellbore integrity and data collection in the energy industry across various regions, with a market cap of CA$87.66 million.

Operations: The company's revenue primarily comes from its Energy Products & Services (EP&S) segment, which generated CA$83.45 million.

Market Cap: CA$87.66M

McCoy Global, with a market cap of CA$87.66 million, operates debt-free and has stable weekly volatility. Its seasoned management and board contribute to its strategic direction. Despite a decline in quarterly sales to CA$14.83 million from the previous year, McCoy remains profitable with net income of CA$0.554 million for Q3 2025. The company's short-term assets cover both its short- and long-term liabilities comfortably, indicating financial stability. However, profit margins have decreased slightly over the past year, and dividend sustainability is questionable due to insufficient free cash flow coverage despite regular payouts being maintained.

Simply Wall St Financial Health Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Vulcan Minerals Inc. focuses on acquiring, evaluating, and exploring mineral properties in Newfoundland and Labrador with a market cap of CA$32.33 million.

Operations: Currently, there are no reported revenue segments for this company.

Market Cap: CA$32.33M

Vulcan Minerals, with a market cap of CA$32.33 million, operates debt-free and has not experienced shareholder dilution in the past year. The company is pre-revenue but recently became profitable, reporting net income for Q3 2025 compared to a loss the previous year. Its short-term assets exceed both short- and long-term liabilities, highlighting financial stability. Recent updates on the Colchester project revealed promising copper drill results, suggesting potential resource expansion opportunities. However, Vulcan's earnings have been impacted by significant one-off items and its return on equity remains low at 0.6%.

data.title: TSX Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership In January 2026
data.description:
As the Canadian market navigates a landscape filled with geopolitical tensions and policy shifts, fundamentals such as low energy prices and moderating inflation continue to provide a stable foundation, allowing the TSX to reach record highs. In this environment, growth companies with high insider ownership can be particularly appealing, as they often signal confidence from those closest to the business and may benefit from resilient economic conditions.

We'll examine a selection from our screener results.

Obsidian Energy (TSX:OBE)

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Obsidian Energy Ltd. focuses on the exploration, development, and production of oil and natural gas in Western Canada, with a market cap of approximately CA$594.65 million.

Operations: The company generates revenue primarily from its oil and gas exploration and production segment, amounting to CA$614.70 million.

Insider Ownership: 10.1%

Revenue Growth Forecast: 18.8% p.a.

Obsidian Energy demonstrates potential as a growth company with substantial insider ownership, evidenced by significant insider buying over the past three months. The company's earnings are forecast to grow at a robust 112.94% annually, outpacing the broader Canadian market's revenue growth. Recent strategic financial maneuvers include closing a $175 million offering of senior unsecured notes, which helped optimize debt structure and reduce borrowing costs. Despite past revenue declines, Obsidian is poised for profitability within three years.

  • Dive into the specifics of Obsidian Energy here with our thorough growth forecast report.
  • Insights from our recent valuation report point to the potential undervaluation of Obsidian Energy shares in the market.

VersaBank (TSX:VBNK)

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: VersaBank offers a range of banking products and services in Canada and the United States, with a market capitalization of CA$685.87 million.

Operations: The company's revenue segments include Digital Meteor at CA$2.21 million, Digital Banking USA at CA$13.00 million, Digital Banking Canada at CA$99.17 million, and DRTC (cybersecurity services and banking and financial technology development) at CA$7.25 million.

Insider Ownership: 11.2%

Revenue Growth Forecast: 27.5% p.a.

VersaBank shows promise with high insider ownership and forecasts of significant earnings growth at 51.19% annually, surpassing the Canadian market average. The bank's revenue is also expected to grow rapidly at 27.5% per year, despite a decline in profit margins from 34.6% to 23.7%. Recent leadership changes include John Asma's promotion and Nicolas Ospina's appointment as CFO, bringing extensive financial expertise to support strategic initiatives in digital banking operations.

  • Click to explore a detailed breakdown of our findings in VersaBank's earnings growth report.
  • Upon reviewing our latest valuation report, VersaBank's share price might be too optimistic.

WELL Health Technologies (TSX:WELL)

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: WELL Health Technologies Corp. is a practitioner-focused digital healthcare company operating in Canada, the United States, and internationally, with a market cap of CA$1.02 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue segments include CA$85.16 million from SaaS and Technology Services, CA$253.13 million from Canadian Patient Services - Primary WMC, CA$160.98 million from Canadian Patient Services - Specialized WDC, CA$116.57 million from WELL Health USA Patient Services - Primary WISP, CA$126.10 million from WELL Health USA Patient Services - Primary Circle Medical, and CA$250.86 million and CA$190.80 million respectively from WELL Health USA Patient Services - Specialized CRH Medical and Provider Staffing.

Insider Ownership: 22.6%

Revenue Growth Forecast: 11.4% p.a.

WELL Health Technologies demonstrates growth potential with insider ownership and a forecasted revenue increase of 11.4% annually, outpacing the Canadian market. The company is expected to become profitable within three years, with earnings projected to grow significantly at 91.86% per year. Recent strategic alliances and acquisitions, including a joint venture for clinical research, aim to enhance its AI and software initiatives while reaffirming strong revenue guidance between C$1.40 billion to C$1.45 billion for 2025.

  • Unlock comprehensive insights into our analysis of WELL Health Technologies stock in this growth report.

data.title: E-Commerce Update - Austrian Market Growth Driven By Innovation And Localization
data.description:
The Austrian B2C e-commerce market is projected to grow steadily at an annual rate of 6.6%, expected to reach $19.22 billion by 2025. This growth is attributed to the increasing focus on mobile-first experiences, alternative delivery solutions, and sustainability initiatives. Despite significant foreign marketplace dominance, domestic retailers are encouraged to differentiate through service innovation and localized offerings to handle ongoing competitive pressures. In 2024, online sales accounted for roughly €10.6 billion, with key sectors being clothing, consumer electronics, and furniture. With regulations on cross-border competition tightening, Austrian merchants are also enhancing local fulfillment and payment capabilities to sustain growth.

In other market news, Ferretti (SEHK:9638) was trading firmly up 17.6% and finishing the session at HK$40.00. At the same time, Microalliance Group (OTCPK:MALG) lagged, down 35.9% to end trading at $1.14.

Adobe's rapid growth potential lies in its AI-driven innovations like Firefly and GenStudio. Click to explore more about Adobe's transformative journey and opportunities.

  • Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) closed at $221.58 up 0.7%, near its 52-week low.
  • Amazon.com (NasdaqGS:AMZN) finished trading at $231.31 up 0.1%.
